rationale: ETH-USD: worst signals include crypto_macro (17.6%), custom_lora (28.9%), funding (29.9%), oscillators (34.5%), volatility_sig (38.7%). Best signals: econ_calendar (72.3%), claude_fundamental (59.9%), structure (59.8%). XAG-USD: worst signals include sentiment (4.5%), ministral (18.9%), oscillators (35.1%). Best signals: fear_greed (93.8%), econ_calendar (78.9%), momentum_factors (77.1%). Both in ranging regime. Focus: identify missing predictive features and explain why certain signals fail catastrophically.

key_predictive_features: ETF Flow Momentum — ETH ETFs saw $71M inflow recently. ETF flow direction (3-day cumulative net inflow/outflow) correlates with 1-3 day price direction. Institutional flows are now a leading indicator, not a lagging one. Track via CoinGlass API or scrape SoSoValue.

Active Addresses (daily) — Mainnet hit record 200.4M transactions in Q1 2026, 43% QoQ jump. Active monthly addresses at 10.4M record. Rising active addresses = rising network utility = price support. Fetch via Etherscan API or Dune Analytics.

Exchange Reserve Change Rate — Exchange reserves dropped to yearly low of 14.9M ETH. 7-day rate of change in exchange reserves is a supply-side leading indicator. Declining reserves = less sell pressure = bullish. Source: CryptoQuant or Glassnode.

Staking Inflows Net — Staking inflows overtook exits for first time in 6 months. Ethereum Foundation staked record 22,517 ETH ($46M) on Mar 30. Net staking flow direction is a medium-term supply lock indicator. Source: Dune Analytics staking dashboard.

Gas Usage Rate of Change — Gas usage aligns closely with network-driven price movements per MDPI research. Rising gas usage = DeFi/NFT activity surge = price support. But relationship is complex: price rises are associated with DECLINING gas prices (efficiency gains). Use 7-day gas usage trend as a composite with active addresses.
DeFi TVL Change Rate — Ethereum hosts $45B+ in TVL. Week-over-week TVL change captures capital flow into/out of the ecosystem. Rising TVL = locked capital = bullish. Source: DefiLlama API (free).

BTC-ETH Correlation Regime — BTC-ETH correlation at 0.89 (near all-time high). When correlation drops below 0.80, ETH is moving on its own fundamentals (bullish divergence). Track rolling 30-day correlation and flag decorrelation events.
ETH/BTC Ratio Momentum — ETH surging 50% while BTC stable signals sector rotation. ETH/BTC ratio 20-day ROC captures this. Rising ratio = altcoin season beginning = ETH outperformance phase.

Layer 2 Transaction Share — L2s process >60% of ETH transactions. Rising L2 share = scaling working = network value accrual. But also means less gas burn on mainnet. Net effect is ambiguous short-term but structurally bullish.

Fed Rate Cut Probability — Rate cut timing is the #1 swing factor per multiple analysts. ETH is more rate-sensitive than BTC due to its 'tech stock' correlation. CME FedWatch tool or FRED futures pricing.
fundamental_drivers: ETF institutional adoption: ETH ETFs saw 177% asset surge in one quarter. Spot ETH ETFs are shifting from speculative vehicle to long-term portfolio allocation. $71M single-day inflow shows structural demand, not just momentum chasing.

Network upgrades pipeline: Ethereum Foundation 'Strawmap' outlines ~7 hard forks through 2029. Immediate: Glamsterdam (H1 2026) and Hegota (H2 2026) targeting gas limit beyond 100M and faster single-slot finality. Each upgrade is a catalyst event.
DeFi ecosystem depth: $45B+ TVL, Uniswap $2.1B daily volume, liquid staking derivatives 18% of TVL, stablecoins on Ethereum exceed $92B. ETH is the settlement layer for the largest crypto economy.

Supply dynamics: Exchange reserves at yearly low (14.9M ETH), staking locking 36M+ ETH, EIP-1559 burn reducing net issuance. Post-merge ETH is deflationary in high-activity periods. Structural supply reduction is ongoing.

L2 scaling success: Arbitrum 46M monthly txns, Optimism 32M. Proto-danksharding (EIP-4844) slashed L2 fees 90%. This solves the scalability trilemma and expands addressable market for DeFi/gaming/social applications.

Ranging regime explanation: ETH at $2,058 is in the lower portion of the 2026 forecast range ($1,900-$4,500). The ranging regime likely reflects macro uncertainty (Fed policy, ETF flow volatility) offsetting strong fundamentals. Resolution direction depends on macro catalyst.
Competition risk: Solana and other L1s gaining market share in transaction volume. ETH dominance in DeFi TVL declining slowly. This is a headwind for pure network-activity-based signals.

cross_asset_correlations.BTC-USD: PRIMARY DRIVER. Correlation at 0.89. ETH follows BTC on macro moves but can diverge during alt-seasons. The recent 50% ETH surge while BTC stable signals potential decorrelation phase. Monitor ETH/BTC ratio as the key relative signal.
cross_asset_correlations.SPY/QQQ: STRONG POSITIVE. ETH trades like a high-beta tech stock. Correlation with QQQ ~0.65 in 2025-2026. Fed rate cut expectations directly impact ETH via tech-stock channel. This explains why crypto_macro signal is at 17.6% — it may be using wrong macro features.
cross_asset_correlations.DXY (US Dollar Index): MODERATE INVERSE. Weaker dollar = capital flows to risk assets including crypto. But ETH is less DXY-sensitive than gold/silver. DXY matters more for BTC (safe haven narrative) than ETH.
cross_asset_correlations.10Y Treasury Yield: MODERATE INVERSE. Rising yields hurt ETH disproportionately vs BTC due to tech-stock correlation. Duration-sensitive like growth equities.
cross_asset_correlations.Gold (XAU-USD): WEAKLY INVERSE in risk-on regimes (rotation from safe haven to risk), WEAKLY POSITIVE in inflation-hedge narrative. Correlation is unstable and regime-dependent.
cross_asset_correlations.VIX: INVERSE. High VIX = risk-off = ETH sells off. Low VIX = risk-on = ETH rallies. VIX spike is a useful short-term sell signal for ETH.
cross_asset_correlations.SOL-USD: COMPETITIVE PROXY. When SOL outperforms ETH, capital is rotating to higher-beta L1s. ETH/SOL ratio declining = ETH underperformance risk.

seasonal_patterns: Q1 (Jan-Mar): Historically strong for crypto. ETH typically rallies in Q1 post-BTC halving years (2017, 2021, 2025). Q1 2026 saw the 50% surge, consistent with this pattern.
April: Transitional month. 'Sell in May' effect beginning to price in. But April 2021 and April 2025 were both positive for ETH. Tax selling in US (April 15) can create temporary weakness.
May-June: Historically weakest period for crypto. 'Sell in May and go away' has ~60% hit rate for ETH. But 2026 may diverge if ETF flows provide structural bid.
July-August: Summer doldrums. Low volume, range-bound. Volatility compression often precedes Q4 breakout.
Sep-Dec: Historically strongest period. Q4 is typically the best quarter for crypto in post-halving years. ETH Q4 2017: +2,000%, Q4 2021: +30%. But diminishing returns each cycle.
papers_and_models: ScienceDirect 'Machine learning Ethereum cryptocurrency prediction and knowledge-based investment strategies' (2024) — Uses ReliefF feature selection. Key: macro-economy factors, Ethereum-specific blockchain info, and OTHER cryptocurrency blockchain info all play important roles. Suggests we need BTC on-chain metrics as ETH inputs.

ScienceDirect 'Data-driven price trends prediction of Ethereum: A hybrid machine learning and signal processing approach' (2024) — Combines ANFIS with Short-Time Fourier Transform. Achieves 76.56% accuracy on trend prediction. STFT captures frequency-domain patterns invisible to standard technical indicators.

ACM 'Machine Learning-Driven Multi-Factor Quantitative Model: A Study on the Ethereum Market' (2025) — Combines RSI, MACD (traditional), gas usage, active addresses (on-chain), and X platform social sentiment (social). Multi-factor approach outperforms single-domain models.

IEEE 'Ether Price Prediction Using Advanced Deep Learning Models' (2021) — GRU and LSTM outperform traditional ML for ETH. 7-day and 15-day horizons have better accuracy than 1-day (noise reduction). Our 3h and 1d horizons may be too noisy.

ArXiv 'Transformer-based approach for Ethereum Price Prediction' (2024) — Transformer architecture captures long-range dependencies in ETH price series better than LSTM. Attention mechanism reveals which past time steps matter most.

SSRN 'Ethereum Price Prediction Using Deep Learning' (2025) — Confirms that incorporating social media sentiment and on-chain metrics alongside price data improves prediction accuracy by 8-12% over price-only models.

recommended_new_signals.name: ETF Flow Momentum Signal
recommended_new_signals.description: Track 3-day cumulative net ETH ETF inflow/outflow. Positive cumulative flow >$50M over 3 days = BUY. Negative cumulative <-$50M = SELL. ETF flows are now the primary institutional demand signal for ETH. Source: CoinGlass API or SoSoValue scrape.
recommended_new_signals.expected_accuracy: 55-60% based on ETF flow-price lag analysis
recommended_new_signals.data_source: CoinGlass API (free tier) or scrape SoSoValue
recommended_new_signals.priority: HIGH — ETF flows are the single most actionable new feature for ETH

recommended_new_signals.name: On-Chain Activity Composite Signal
recommended_new_signals.description: Combine active addresses (7d MA), gas usage (7d MA), and exchange reserve change (7d) into a single composite score using z-score normalization. All three rising = BUY (network growth + supply contraction). All three declining = SELL. Mixed = HOLD. Source: Etherscan API (free) + CryptoQuant.
recommended_new_signals.data_source: Etherscan API (free), CryptoQuant (may need API key)
recommended_new_signals.priority: HIGH — fills the biggest gap in our ETH signal coverage

recommended_new_signals.name: ETH/BTC Ratio Regime Signal
recommended_new_signals.description: Compute ETH/BTC 20-day ROC. When ETH/BTC ratio rising >5% over 20d = alt-season starting = BUY ETH. When declining >5% = BTC dominance phase = SELL ETH. Decorrelation events (rolling 30d corr drops below 0.75) are high-conviction directional signals.
recommended_new_signals.data_source: Already available from Binance data (ETH and BTC prices)
recommended_new_signals.priority: MEDIUM — easy to implement, leverages existing data

recommended_new_signals.name: Staking Flow Direction Signal
recommended_new_signals.description: Track net ETH staking flow (deposits minus withdrawals) on 7-day rolling basis. Persistent net positive flow = supply locking = medium-term bullish. Net negative flow = unlocking/selling = bearish. This is a slow-moving but high-conviction structural signal.
recommended_new_signals.data_source: Dune Analytics query (free) or Beaconcha.in API
recommended_new_signals.priority: MEDIUM — structural signal, not timing signal

recommended_new_signals.name: Crypto Macro Regime Fix
recommended_new_signals.description: CRITICAL: Our crypto_macro signal is at 17.6% accuracy — worse than random. Root cause hypothesis: it likely uses wrong macro features for ETH. ETH correlates with QQQ (tech stocks), not traditional macro. Fix: replace current macro features with QQQ momentum, VIX level, and Fed funds futures probability. This is a FIX, not a new signal.
recommended_new_signals.priority: CRITICAL — 17.6% accuracy means this signal is actively hurting us

signal_failure_analysis.crypto_macro_17pct: At 17.6%, this signal is ANTI-predictive. It is likely using traditional macro indicators (DXY, yields, commodities) that have an unstable relationship with ETH. ETH behaves more like a high-beta tech stock than a macro asset. The fix is to recalibrate macro features to QQQ correlation, VIX regime, and rate-cut probability rather than raw yield/DXY levels.
signal_failure_analysis.custom_lora_28pct: Custom LoRA has 97% SELL bias (documented in CLAUDE.md). At 28.9% it is actively harmful. Should remain DISABLED. The fine-tuning data was contaminated with bearish bias from training on the 2022-2023 bear market.
signal_failure_analysis.funding_29pct: Funding rate contrarian logic is backwards for ETH in this regime. In a ranging market, negative funding = retail short = BUT in ranging markets shorts are correct more often than in trending markets. The contrarian assumption fails in range-bound conditions. Should add regime filter: only apply contrarian funding in trending regime.
signal_failure_analysis.oscillators_34pct: Traditional oscillators (Stochastic, CCI, Williams %R, etc.) fail in ranging markets because they generate constant overbought/oversold signals that whipsaw. In ranging regime, oscillators should use wider thresholds (e.g., RSI <20 / >80 instead of <30 / >70) or be suppressed entirely.
signal_failure_analysis.volatility_sig_38pct: Volatility signals (BB Squeeze, ATR Expansion, Keltner) are designed to predict breakouts. In a ranging market, they fire false breakout signals repeatedly. The fix is a regime gate: suppress volatility breakout signals when ADX < 20 (confirming range).

deep_dives.implementation_notes: ETH signal accuracy is severely degraded by 3 catastrophic signals (crypto_macro 17.6%, custom_lora 28.9%, funding 29.9%) that are actively anti-predictive. The IMMEDIATE priority is: (1) disable or fix crypto_macro by switching to QQQ/VIX-based features instead of traditional macro, (2) keep custom_lora disabled, (3) add regime filter to funding signal to suppress contrarian logic in ranging markets, (4) add ETF flow momentum as new signal since institutional flows are now ETH's primary price driver. The best-performing signals (econ_calendar 72.3%, claude_fundamental 59.9%, structure 59.8%) succeed because they capture structural/fundamental factors that persist across regimes. New signals should follow this pattern: focus on structural supply/demand (on-chain, ETF flows, staking) rather than mean-reversion technicals which fail in range.

key_predictive_features: Gold-Silver Ratio (GSR) z-score — Historical mean 60-70:1. The '80/60 rule': GSR >80 = buy silver, GSR <60 = sell silver. Recent ML research (SSRN 2026) shows hybrid cointegration + Kalman filter + ML classifier improves GSR mean-reversion timing. GSR is the single most reliable structural signal for XAG direction.

DXY (US Dollar Index) momentum — #1 predictor per SHAP analysis (confirmed again today). Strong inverse correlation with silver. 20-day ROC of DXY captures the primary macro force on silver. We STILL don't have this as an explicit signal input.

Real Yields (10Y TIPS, DFII10) — Non-yielding silver inversely correlated with real rates. Falling real yields reduce opportunity cost of holding silver. #3 predictor per SHAP. Track via FRED API (already integrated for treasury yields).

COMEX Managed Money Net Positioning — COT report. Net long >60-70K contracts = overextended, risk of liquidation cascade. Net long near 0 or negative = washout, contrarian buy. Silver futures market is small, so positioning shifts of 10-20K contracts move price meaningfully.

COMEX Silver Inventory Change Rate — 4-week rate of change in registered COMEX silver inventory. Declining inventory = tightening physical market = bullish. 6th consecutive year of structural deficit in 2026.

Copper Momentum (1-2 week lead) — Industrial metals co-move. Copper leads silver by 1-2 weeks. Already in metals_cross_asset.py but uses static 1.5% threshold. Should switch to rolling 10-day correlation and adaptive threshold.
Solar Installation Run Rate — 160M oz silver consumption from solar alone in 2026. Quarterly reporting from Silver Institute / IEA. Rising installation rate = rising structural demand floor.

All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) Floor — Global weighted avg AISC at $23.44/oz in 2026. 70% of silver is byproduct from copper/lead/zinc mining, so silver price alone cannot incentivize supply response. AISC provides a fundamental price floor signal.

GVZ (Gold Volatility Index) — Already tracked in metals_cross_assets.py. High GVZ = uncertainty in precious metals = range expansion expected. Low GVZ = compression = breakout imminent. Useful for position sizing, not direction.

Fear & Greed Index — Currently our BEST signal for XAG at 93.8% accuracy. Contrarian application works exceptionally well for silver because silver is more sentiment-driven than gold. Extreme fear = BUY silver aggressively.
fundamental_drivers: Structural supply deficit: 67M oz deficit forecast for 2026, 6th consecutive deficit year. Mine output ~830M oz/year growing only 1-2% annually while industrial consumption rises 4-6% annually. This is a multi-year structural imbalance.

Solar PV demand: 160M oz in 2026. TOPCon and heterojunction cells use MORE silver than PERC. Copper substitution attempts face reliability issues and are not viable before 2028. Solar alone is 19% of total silver demand.
EV silver consumption: Modern EVs require ~50g silver per vehicle (2x ICE vehicles). EV vehicles will overtake ICE as primary automotive silver demand source by 2027. Growing at 15-20% annually.

AI/Data Center demand: Emerging demand driver. Silver used in high-conductivity connectors, thermal management, and circuit boards. Data center buildout for AI is accelerating silver demand in electronics sector.

Supply constraint: 70% of silver is byproduct from copper, lead, zinc, gold mining. Silver price alone CANNOT incentivize faster supply response. This is a unique structural constraint — unlike gold, higher prices don't directly increase silver mine supply.

AISC cost floor: Global average AISC at $23.44/oz. Mexico (largest producer) at $19.84/oz, Peru at $25.16/oz, Poland at $34.44/oz. With silver at $73, all producers are profitable, but the cost floor prevents supply collapse and provides downside support.
Central bank gold buying: Supports gold price floor, which supports silver via GSR compression. Gold at record highs creates a structural tailwind for silver.
Fed dovish stance: Expected rate cuts reduce opportunity cost of holding non-yielding silver. Inverse real yield relationship remains the primary macro driver.

cross_asset_correlations.Gold (XAU-USD): PRIMARY DRIVER. Silver follows gold with 1.5-2x beta. GSR mean-reverts from extremes (80/60 rule). When gold rallies, silver typically outperforms in late-stage moves. Gold at record highs is the strongest single tailwind for silver.
cross_asset_correlations.DXY (US Dollar Index): STRONG INVERSE. #1 macro predictor per SHAP analysis. DXY decline since Q3 2025 was a primary driver of the 147% XAG rally. This is the most critical missing input in our signal system.
cross_asset_correlations.Copper (HG=F): LEADING INDICATOR (1-2 week lead). Industrial metals co-move on demand expectations. Copper-gold ratio sitting on historical support suggests copper (and by extension silver's industrial component) could outperform in 2026. Already tracked but needs adaptive threshold.
cross_asset_correlations.Real Yields (DFII10): STRONG INVERSE. #3 predictor per SHAP. When real yields fall from 2.5% to 1.5%, silver typically rallies 15-25%. The real yield-silver relationship is more stable than DXY-silver.
cross_asset_correlations.Oil (CL=F): INDIRECT via inflation expectations. Oil spike = inflation hedge bid = precious metals bid. Effect is mediated, not direct. Already tracked in metals_cross_asset.py.
cross_asset_correlations.VIX: REGIME-DEPENDENT. Mild positive in crisis (safe haven), negative in risk-on (industrial optimism). Mixed signal, low conviction. Use as regime indicator, not directional signal.
cross_asset_correlations.SPY: WEAKLY POSITIVE. Risk-on supports industrial metals demand optimism. But silver can rally in risk-off too (safe haven). Ambiguous correlation, low signal value.

seasonal_patterns: January-March: STRONG. Industrial stockpiling for spring manufacturing and solar production season. Silver outperforms gold in Q1. The bulk of 2026's rally occurred in this window.

April: TRANSITIONAL with IMPROVING recent pattern. Traditional seasonal models show weakness (April-June decline), but recent years show 80% probability of April price increase due to external factors (stimulus, supply deficits, industrial demand). Current XAG at $73 entering April is historically positive.
Mid-April dip: Quarterly data shows ~0.5% decline mid-month compared to April open. Short-term tactical: expect a small dip around April 10-15 before month-end recovery.

May-June: HISTORICALLY WEAK. 'Sell in May' effect applies to metals. Manufacturing orders already placed; physical demand slows. The seasonal headwind is real but can be overridden by structural deficit dynamics.
July-August: MIXED. Summer doldrums but safe-haven spikes possible during geopolitical events. Low volume means positioning-driven moves are amplified.
September-December: STRONG. Jewelry demand (India Diwali, Chinese New Year prep), year-end portfolio rebalancing, industrial restocking. Historically the best period for silver.
papers_and_models: SSRN 'Gold Silver Pair Trading: Mean Reversion Strategy Using Machine Learning' (2026) — Hybrid framework integrating cointegration, Kalman filtering, and ML classifiers for GSR mean reversion. ML-filtered entries outperform static statistical arbitrage by improving timing and reducing drawdowns. Directly applicable to our GSR signal.

SSRN 'Forecasting precious metals price based on artificial neural network trained by Levy flight optimization' — ANN with Levy flight optimizer outperforms standard gradient-based training for precious metals. Non-standard optimization helps escape local minima in volatile silver price series.

ScienceDirect 'Silver Price Forecasting Using XGBoost' — Top features by SHAP importance: MA200, WAD, MA20, oil volatility, silver volatility. XGBoost outperforms CatBoost and RF. Confirms that trend-following features (MA200) dominate for silver.

Springer 'Forecasting gold price with XGBoost and SHAP' — SHAP interaction values reveal DXY-real yield interaction is the strongest predictor PAIR for precious metals. The interaction (not individual features) matters most.

CNBC 'Gold and silver price swings powering algo traders and ML funds' (Feb 2026) — CTAs using statistical models, ML algorithms, and quantitative signals to trade precious metals. Confirms institutional adoption of ML-driven metals trading.

Silver Institute 'Silver Demand Forecast to Expand Across Key Technology Sectors' (2026) — Solar PV demand may fall 7% YoY despite 15% capacity growth due to silver intensity reduction. But EV and AI/data center demand more than offsets. Net industrial demand still growing 4-6%/year.

recommended_new_signals.name: DXY Momentum Signal
recommended_new_signals.description: Fetch DXY via yfinance ('^DXY') or FRED ('DTWEXBGS'). Compute 20-day rate of change. DXY falling >1% over 20d = BUY silver. DXY rising >1% = SELL. Strongest single macro predictor per SHAP. STILL not implemented despite being recommended yesterday.
recommended_new_signals.priority: CRITICAL — #1 missing predictor, recommended 2 days in a row

recommended_new_signals.name: Real Yield Spread Signal
recommended_new_signals.description: Fetch 10Y TIPS yield from FRED ('DFII10'). Compute 20-day rate of change. Falling real yields = BUY silver. Rising = SELL. DXY-real yield INTERACTION is the strongest predictor pair per SHAP research.

recommended_new_signals.name: ML-Enhanced GSR Signal
recommended_new_signals.description: Replace static GSR z-score with XGBoost classifier using GSR z-score + DXY 20d ROC + real yield 20d ROC + VIX + oil vol as features. Predicts whether GSR deviation will mean-revert within 5 days. Train on 2 years daily data. The 80/60 rule provides the baseline, ML refines timing.
recommended_new_signals.data_source: Already computed in metals_cross_assets.py + new DXY/TIPS inputs
recommended_new_signals.priority: HIGH — directly leverages best academic research

recommended_new_signals.name: COT Positioning Extreme Signal
recommended_new_signals.description: Fetch weekly CFTC COT data for silver (Managed Money net positioning). Flag extremes: net long >60K = overextended/SELL risk, net long near 0 or negative = washout/BUY opportunity. Silver's small futures market makes positioning shifts of 10-20K contracts highly price-impactful.
recommended_new_signals.expected_accuracy: 55-65% on weekly horizon (low frequency, high conviction)
recommended_new_signals.priority: MEDIUM — weekly frequency limits real-time value but high conviction at extremes

recommended_new_signals.name: Sentiment Signal Fix for XAG
recommended_new_signals.description: CRITICAL: Sentiment is at 4.5% accuracy for XAG — catastrophically anti-predictive. Root cause: CryptoBERT/Trading-Hero-LLM sentiment models were trained on crypto/stock narratives, not precious metals. Silver sentiment follows different lexicon (safe haven, inflation hedge, industrial demand, deficit). Options: (a) disable for metals entirely, (b) retrain with metals-specific corpus, (c) replace with COT positioning as sentiment proxy.
recommended_new_signals.effort: 0.5 days to disable, 3 days to retrain
recommended_new_signals.priority: CRITICAL — 4.5% accuracy is actively destroying XAG predictions

signal_failure_analysis.sentiment_4pct: At 4.5%, sentiment is the worst signal in our entire system for any ticker. The NLP models (CryptoBERT, Trading-Hero-LLM) are trained on crypto and stock sentiment corpora. Precious metals sentiment uses completely different vocabulary: 'safe haven', 'inflation hedge', 'supply deficit', 'industrial demand', 'central bank buying'. The models literally do not understand what bullish/bearish means for silver. IMMEDIATE ACTION: disable sentiment for XAG-USD until a metals-specific model is available. Every cycle this signal votes, it actively hurts accuracy.
signal_failure_analysis.ministral_18pct: Ministral-8B at 18.9% for XAG is severely anti-predictive. Likely cause: the local LLM lacks metals market expertise and may be pattern-matching on crypto/stock logic that doesn't transfer. Silver has unique dynamics (byproduct supply constraint, GSR mechanics, industrial/monetary duality) that a general-purpose 8B model cannot reason about. Consider: (a) providing metals-specific context in the prompt, (b) using RAG with Silver Institute reports, (c) suppressing Ministral for metals.
signal_failure_analysis.oscillators_35pct: Traditional oscillators fail for XAG in ranging regime, same as ETH. Stochastic, CCI, Williams %R generate constant overbought/oversold signals that whipsaw in range. Silver's high intraday volatility (2-3% daily moves common) means oscillator thresholds are hit frequently but don't predict direction. Fix: widen thresholds for metals (RSI <15/>85), or suppress oscillators entirely when ADX < 20.
signal_failure_analysis.fear_greed_93pct_explained: Fear & Greed is our BEST signal at 93.8% for XAG. Why? Because F&G measures broad market sentiment extremes, and silver is the most sentiment-sensitive metal. When fear is extreme, silver has been oversold beyond fundamental value (supply deficit puts a floor). When greed is extreme, silver is overbought on momentum. The contrarian application works because silver's fundamental floor (AISC + deficit) creates asymmetric mean reversion — overshoots to the downside are larger and more frequent, making extreme fear a high-conviction BUY. This is our gold standard signal for XAG.

deep_dives.implementation_notes: XAG signal accuracy is bifurcated: excellent structural signals (fear_greed 93.8%, econ_calendar 78.9%, momentum_factors 77.1%, claude_fundamental 73.9%) vs catastrophic technical/NLP signals (sentiment 4.5%, ministral 18.9%, oscillators 35.1%). The structural signals work because they capture supply-demand fundamentals and macro regime that dominate silver pricing. The failed signals apply crypto/equity logic to a unique commodity. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: (1) DISABLE sentiment for XAG (4.5% = actively harmful), (2) DISABLE or add metals context to ministral for XAG (18.9% = actively harmful), (3) ADD DXY momentum signal (recommended 2 days running, still not implemented, #1 SHAP predictor), (4) ADD real yield signal (FRED DFII10, #3 predictor), (5) ADD regime filter to oscillators (suppress when ADX < 20). The combination of disabling 2 anti-predictive signals and adding 2 high-quality macro signals should improve XAG accuracy by an estimated 5-10 percentage points across horizons.

common_themes: Both tickers suffer from signals designed for trending markets being applied in ranging regime. Oscillators, volatility breakout signals, and momentum indicators all fail when price is range-bound.
Both tickers have catastrophically bad NLP/ML signals that are actively anti-predictive. These need to be disabled or regime-gated immediately.

Both tickers' best signals are structural/fundamental (econ_calendar, claude_fundamental, fear_greed, structure) rather than technical. This suggests the ranging regime rewards fundamental analysis over technical pattern-matching.
Both tickers are missing critical macro inputs: ETH needs QQQ/VIX correlation, XAG needs DXY/real yields. Adding the right macro features is higher-impact than adding new technical indicators.
priority_actions: P0 — DISABLE: sentiment for XAG (4.5%), keep custom_lora disabled for ETH (28.9%)
P0 — FIX: crypto_macro for ETH (17.6%) by switching to QQQ/VIX-based features
P0 — ADD: DXY momentum signal for XAG (recommended 2 consecutive days, highest SHAP importance)
P1 — ADD: ETF flow momentum signal for ETH (institutional flows now primary driver)
P1 — ADD: Real yield spread signal for XAG (FRED DFII10, #3 predictor)
P1 — ADD: Regime filter to oscillators for both tickers (suppress when ADX < 20)
P2 — ADD: On-chain activity composite for ETH (active addresses + gas + exchange reserves)
P2 — ADD: ETH/BTC ratio regime signal (decorrelation detection)
P2 — ADD: COT positioning extreme signal for XAG (weekly, high conviction)
P3 — INVESTIGATE: ministral for XAG (18.9%) — add metals-specific context or suppress
meta_analysis.estimated_impact: Disabling 2-3 anti-predictive signals alone should improve ensemble accuracy by 3-5%. Adding DXY and real yield signals for XAG could add another 3-5%. Fixing crypto_macro for ETH could swing it from -32% alpha (worse than random) to neutral or slightly positive. Total estimated improvement: 8-15 percentage points for both tickers over 30-day evaluation period.
